evidence: 'Batman V Superman' is a DC comics fans dream come true.
'It Follows' is easily one of the best horror movies you'll likely see this year.
'BvS' was never meant to be your father's comic book film. It's meant to be darker. Grittier.
'Automata' has a fairly straightforward Story(both fascinating and wholly unoriginal).
'BvS' has been hacked up by the studio to shorten its run time as it's VERY obvious that there are scenes missing throughout the film.
'The Interview' wears somewhat thin, and the comedy often fails to generate real laughs.
I don't see much of an improvement worthy of paying a premium for this collection.
'It Follows' creates tension with an amazing musical score, great atmosphere/camera direction.
I've been a long time defender of director Zach Snyder.
I respect what Guillermo del Toro tried to do.
'It Follows' creates tension with an amazing musical score.
'Batman V Superman' is visually stunning to me.
'Grave of the Fireflies' is a film that reaches emotionally jarring heights.
In all my years of watching Anime films only 2 have been so moving enough to bring me to tears.

summary: The user enjoys a mix of genres, particularly sci-fi, action, and horror, with a strong appreciation for quality production and emotional depth. They show loyalty to specific directors and value narrative complexity, though they have mixed feelings about pacing in films.
